215876.explanation: Roosendaal needs its own homeless shelter that structurally provides a place for residents who unfortunately have to use it. By offering shelter and assistance, people who are open to it can be helped towards a more stable future.

215877.explanation: It is unnecessary to use two locations for this. The city center and the station are almost adjacent. Having one fixed location saves costs and provides the opportunity to discuss matters within the municipality on a daily basis.

215878.explanation: People who have ties to our municipality should be given priority. This stimulates social cohesion, as people who feel connected to our municipality are more likely to continue living within our municipal boundaries and still attend the same clubs, sports associations, etc.

215880.explanation: It is undesirable for buildings to remain empty for a long time; however, a building is the property of a person or entity. It is absurd that a municipality can levy a tax if a building is not occupied in time or as desired. Moreover, the income from vacancy tax does not compensate for the administrative capacity required, i.e., money.

215881.explanation: Small expansions at the edges of the villages are acceptable; however, there are still enough opportunities in Roosendaal itself to build within the city. The rural area should be preserved as much as possible from land loss.

215882.explanation: It is good that houses are optimally insulated because heat that is not lost does not need to be replaced. However, FVD believes that new homes should still be able to be connected to gas.

215883.explanation: There is a need for various types of housing, both large and small. Therefore, it is desirable to build a varied mix of houses and apartments.

215884.explanation: A maximum of 30% social rental housing. It is already very difficult for project developers to realize profitable projects. Raising the minimum standard to 40% would make the municipality of Roosendaal unattractive for project developers to build houses or apartments.

215886.explanation: Employers can obtain subsidies from the national government to help this target group get to work. Currently, there are already opportunities through the employment center and Ministry of Health, Welfare and Sport to help this group find work. FVD considers this sufficient.

215887.explanation: If someone's health permits, this is certainly valuable. A daily program ensures that these people do not become isolated and can contribute to society.

215888.explanation: The UWV (Employee Insurance Agency) is responsible for supporting the unemployed; this is not the municipality's task. FVD is not in favor of creating subsidized jobs with public funds.

215889.explanation: FVD does not advocate a complete stop but for reducing debt forgiveness from 100% to 50%. This lowers the financial burden on other households that are not entitled to all the benefits that this group can and may use.

215890.explanation: There are already various schemes for residents with little money. Every family also needs different assistance. One large budget does not work for everyone. It would be better to provide tailored assistance with good guidance so residents find help that suits them.

215891.explanation: FVD does not see added value in this.

215893.explanation: Parking fees have drastically increased in recent years. This is undesirable. Parking fees should come down again to make the city center attractive for shopping, buying groceries, dining, and visiting family and friends. This supports local entrepreneurs.

215894.explanation: The parking regime has changed significantly in recent years to the detriment of motorists: higher rates, longer paid parking durations, and expanded paid parking zones. In other cities, it is possible to accommodate motorists in certain ways. FVD wants this for Roosendaal as well.

215895.explanation: It is important that there are better times and connections between our and surrounding municipalities. However, FVD believes this should not be financed by the municipalities but arranged by the province and the transport operator.

215896.explanation: Slow down where necessary and drive through where possible. A 30 km/h zone already applies in residential areas. On through roads, the speed limit should remain 50 km/h.

215899.explanation: The current form and range of assistance offered to this group is sufficient. It is also clearly stated on the municipal website and community centers. No additional information campaign needs to be started for this.

215900.explanation: Elderly people deserve attention and a place to come together. Loneliness is a major problem. However, the elderly need more encouragement to get out of the house. Existing neighborhood centers and associations sufficiently facilitate meeting each other. There are already many neighborhood centers and associations.

215842.explanation: Opinions should be asked of residents but not through a referendum; after all, we have a democratically elected municipal council. The opinions of residents, including those from the villages, are very important to Vitaal Liberaal.

215844.explanation: That is what the municipal council is for, democratically elected by the residents.

215845.explanation: There must be a fair valuation.

215847.explanation: Vitaal Liberaal finds cooperation very important.

215848.explanation: Roosendaal is an important logistical hub, but it should be considered on a case-by-case basis. It must add employment.

215850.explanation: There is certainly something to be said for this, but it must be carefully considered by moment and location.

215851.explanation: The more companies, the more jobs. And that is important to Vitaal Liberaal.

215852.explanation: Again important for Vitaal Liberaal: more companies mean more employment.

215853.explanation: Vitaal Liberaal favors equal treatment but wants to examine distressing cases separately.

215854.explanation: Vitaal Liberaal considers good integration vital for newcomers and for our residents.

215855.explanation: Vitaal Liberaal wants Roosendaal to comply with national obligations regarding asylum seekers. But with a well-considered location and in good consultation with the surroundings. Fair reception.

215857.explanation: The related issues must be addressed.

215859.explanation: This encourages people not to dump illegally, but Vitaal Liberaal wants to encourage reducing residual waste.

215860.explanation: Healthy and better for the environment, important to Vitaal Liberaal.

215863.explanation: At least one; green space is important to Vitaal Liberaal.

215865.explanation: Public toilets are mainly a big problem for women; Vitaal Liberaal would like to see a solution for this.

215866.explanation: Agricultural land is for agriculture, and we prefer solar panels on roofs, but within the agreed 75 hectares and preferably with the participation of local residents, it might be possible.

215867.explanation: Vitaal Liberaal considers both sports and culture very important for all children; good for their development.

215868.explanation: Vitaal Liberaal considers both sports and culture very important for all children; good for their development.

215869.explanation: Since school swimming lessons were abolished, too many children cannot swim, which concerns Vitaal Liberaal. We support reintroducing them.

215870.explanation: Vitaal Liberaal also wants to make room for the many amateurs in this field.

215871.explanation: There are already many events, but there still appears to be a need for this. It should be evaluated annually to see if it meets the demand.

215872.explanation: The city can benefit from people who studied here, and Vitaal Liberaal is happy with people who stay here after their studies.

215873.explanation: Safety first, an important issue for Vitaal Liberaal. Currently there is much attention to women's safety, but safety must be ensured for all residents of Roosendaal and the surrounding villages.

215874.explanation: Especially in problem neighborhoods, but with attention to privacy.

215875.explanation: Noise nuisance should preferably be discussed with those causing it, and only then possibly fined.

215876.explanation: Vitaal Liberaal does want to look into why someone is homeless and try to address the problems.

215877.explanation: A fixed location is good for sharing experiences. But preferably start with one location and evaluate its usefulness.

215878.explanation: Residents from the villages should be given priority in those villages, and similarly in the neighborhoods. This depends on circumstances though.

215880.explanation: Combating vacancy is an important issue for Vitaal Liberaal.

215881.explanation: Addressing the housing shortage is an important issue for Vitaal Liberaal, but with attention to green spaces and the environment. Consider infill or ribbon development as well.

215882.explanation: Vitaal Liberaal mainly wants to stimulate energy-neutral building; the word 'mandatory' is too strong. So preferably build environmentally friendly.

215883.explanation: Vitaal Liberaal pays a lot of attention to first-time buyers, to elderly people who want to downsize, and thus to housing flow. Build according to demand.

215884.explanation: Vitaal Liberaal does not believe Roosendaal should build more social rental housing than the Dutch average. Build according to demand.

215886.explanation: These people also deserve a respectful and proper existence according to Vitaal Liberaal. Moreover, it can help reduce the labor shortage; it does not necessarily have to cost money.

215887.explanation: Appropriate social work can be meaningful for individuals and society, and Vitaal Liberaal pays full attention to that.

215888.explanation: Vitaal Liberaal prefers stimulating unemployed people to learn and find a 'real' job.

215889.explanation: Help residents with low incomes to manage their finances in a positively encouraging way.

215890.explanation: Help people with low income and debts gain oversight, preferably with a one-time payment and then further help to move on.

215891.explanation: Vitaal Liberaal sees this as encouraging activity. It must be well-lit and safe though.

215893.explanation: Preferably balanced rates spread across the city.

215894.explanation: Vitaal Liberaal sees this as a good way to support entrepreneurs and attract people to shop on Sundays.

215895.explanation: Vitaal Liberaal wants a thorough evaluation of the entire public transport offer and adjustments where necessary.

215899.explanation: Too many people do not know the right way to find support; this needs improvement.

215900.explanation: Where necessary, Vitaal Liberaal supports this.

215842.explanation: A majority in the city council does not always mean that the majority of residents agree with the plans. When new plans have major consequences for all residents, they must have the opportunity to participate in the decisions. That can increase trust in politics.

215844.explanation: It is the task of city council members as representatives to be informed about what matters to residents. An additional advisory body undermines the council's function. Besides, it is questionable how balanced the advice will be and whether the council may deviate from it.

215845.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is the only tax that the municipality levies to carry out all municipal tasks. In Roosendaal it is not higher than in comparable municipalities. Residents and businesses have demands on the municipality, and sufficient financial means are needed for that.

215847.explanation: Collaboration with other municipalities can be efficient and yield better quality. These collaborative organizations must remain under the control of the councils of the collaborating municipalities. They have limited autonomy and report directly to the municipalities.

215848.explanation: The number of logistics companies and the space they occupy is now disproportionately large. Many labor migrants also work at these companies, which causes housing problems, for example. A more varied business structure offers better job opportunities for residents.

215849.explanation: Temporary stores are a superficial solution. Moreover, other problems may arise, such as criminal fronts. Vacancy can be solved by municipal policy requiring commercial and retail properties vacant for more than two years to receive a different purpose.

215850.explanation: Parking fees are high in Roosendaal. Additionally, shopkeepers struggle to attract customers due to high vacancy rates and poor accessibility. Parking at Oostplein and neighborhood shopping centers is free, resulting in heavy parking pressure there. Shopping in the city should be made more attractive.

215851.explanation: There are enough companies, but too little variety in business activity. Renovating outdated business parks and tackling vacancy and derelict sites will improve attractiveness.

215852.explanation: There is no employment problem in Roosendaal, but rather a mismatch between business activities and workers. Existing companies should make greater use of schemes and opportunities for young people and those with a distance to the labor market.

215853.explanation: Housing is a right for all residents of this country. No distinction is made based on origin or situation. The real problem is the shortage of social rental housing, which can be solved with several drastic measures.

215854.explanation: Integration of newcomers brings many benefits for both the newcomers themselves and society: more mutual understanding, more participation. Companies should be required to offer their labor migrants (at their own expense) an integration course.

215855.explanation: All Dutch municipalities must offer sufficient reception places based on the distribution law. The municipality of Roosendaal must actively seek a suitable location that is easily accessible with existing facilities. Request information from municipalities with positive experiences with a reception center (azc).

215857.explanation: The closure of coffee shops has led to the dilapidation of buildings and the displacement of drug problems to nearly all neighborhoods in Roosendaal. Soft drugs are legal and meet a need for certain people. Legal sale at a few locations prevents nuisance throughout the municipality.

215859.explanation: The principle of 'the polluter pays' ensures that residents manage waste more consciously. Most waste streams are processed for free, so conscious waste separation is rewarded.

215860.explanation: Greenery ensures a better living environment and more biodiversity: good for people and animals. Moreover, it helps prevent water nuisance and heat stress.

215863.explanation: Trees are indispensable for livability. There are already too few trees in industrial areas and parking lots. Therefore, besides replacement, expanding the number of trees is important. It is better to plant 2 new trees for every tree cut down.

215865.explanation: There are too few public toilets in the city and villages. A hospitable municipality should take into account all the needs of its residents and visitors. Entrepreneurs also have an interest in this and can contribute by providing funds or making toilets available for all visitors.

215866.explanation: Not only on agricultural land but also in industrial areas. Space is scarce and must be used optimally. As long as not all roofs (especially of commercial buildings) are utilized, it is unnecessary to use scarce space for this purpose.

215867.explanation: Sports and culture connect people. Children who come into contact with sports and culture at a young age will likely remain active in them as adults. This can lead to more active members in clubs and more volunteers.

215868.explanation: Neighborhood sports coaches are important in districts and villages. Besides their own activities, they can also encourage their participants to join existing (sports) associations.

215869.explanation: In a water-rich country like the Netherlands, it is necessary that all children learn to swim. Swimming lessons are expensive, and not all parents can afford them or see their necessity. Group swimming lessons also ensure better use of the swimming pool.

215870.explanation: Art is important but only one of the possible uses of public space.

215871.explanation: There are already pop venues in Bergen op Zoom and Breda. Additionally, open-air festivals are organized. First, the demand for this must be investigated. The temporary Kring (EKP building) could possibly be suitable for this.

215872.explanation: Providing higher education is not an end in itself. If Roosendaal focuses on a broader vocational education (MBO) with sufficient internships and employment, this also has an attractive effect on young people.

215873.explanation: Enforcement is very important, but more enforcement officers does not automatically make things safer. More collaboration between enforcement officers (BOA's) and neighborhood watch teams in districts and villages is likely to increase safety. In the city center and rural areas, the police should be more visible.

215874.explanation: Camera surveillance barely provides extra security. At locations where attacks were regularly committed, the attacks continued afterward. Moreover, the privacy of all residents is sacrificed for a few (minor) criminals acting on behalf of big bosses who remain out of sight.

215875.explanation: Noise nuisance is a major source of annoyance, but little action is taken due to capacity shortages. It is more important that reports are taken seriously and that actual enforcement can take place.

215876.explanation: Homelessness is a structural problem due to too few available homes and because labor migrants lose their work and (at the same time) housing. As long as the causes are not resolved, homeless people must be given shelter in their immediate surroundings, also for everyone's safety.

215877.explanation: Enforcement officers (BOA's) should be present on the streets as much as possible, aside from the fixed posts they currently have.

215878.explanation: Housing seekers who are socially and economically tied to the municipality are given priority. This assessment must be based on objective criteria. To prevent price inflation, a system can be introduced for owner-occupied homes whereby homes must first be offered locally.

215880.explanation: Vacancy causes insecurity and neighborhood decay. In addition, vacant properties can be converted into homes, as is currently happening in Dr. Braberstraat. By taxing vacancy, property owners will be more likely to seek a better use.

215881.explanation: There are many empty spots in the city and villages where housing construction is possible. Additionally, many vacant buildings can be converted into homes. By offering this option, existing places are not affected. The situation may vary per village.

215882.explanation: Sustainable and as energy-efficient building as possible is important, but this should not lead to fewer homes being built. Imposing high requirements for new homes currently makes the difference with existing homes too large. Especially the existing social rental homes must therefore be made sustainable more quickly.

215883.explanation: Affordable, smaller owner-occupied and rental homes and apartments must be built in large numbers and with priority. The municipality takes charge, for example, through permit issuance. Older people moving to an apartment promote throughput in favor of younger families.

215884.explanation: For more and more people, owning a home is an unattainable ideal. Moreover, in the last 25 years, too few rental homes have been built. Given waiting lists of 6 to 7 years, the construction of social rental homes cannot go fast enough.

215886.explanation: Funds are available from the National Program for Livability and Safety. These can be used for projects that increase labor participation. The municipality itself can also set a good example by offering jobs to people with disabilities.

215887.explanation: A social assistance benefit is a minimal income provision for people who cannot work or temporarily have no income. When these people are put to work, they can be paid for that work and are no longer dependent on social assistance.

215888.explanation: Given the number of labor migrants, there is sufficient employment in the municipality. Companies offering jobs to the long-term unemployed already receive benefits. The job center can ensure that these long-term unemployed are matched to jobs with extra subsidy from the municipality.

215889.explanation: As long as wages and benefits are too low to guarantee everyone a sufficient income, the debt relief scheme is necessary: a necessary measure to ensure basic security.

215890.explanation: Wages and benefits are too low to provide basic security for everyone. When the many arrangements for low-income people are bundled, help reaches those entitled to it more quickly. Currently, there is much unawareness about the arrangements, so the money is not utilized.

215891.explanation: It has not been proven that fast cycling routes reduce car traffic. Improving public transport yields more results and prevents new roads from having to be built in rural areas.

215893.explanation: Alongside the first hour free parking, this makes shopping in Roosendaal's center more attractive. Additionally, parking garages are better utilized and parking pressure in neighborhoods outside the center decreases.

215894.explanation: Alongside the first hour free parking, this makes shopping in Roosendaal's center more attractive. Additionally, parking garages are better utilized.

215895.explanation: Public transport to villages, districts, and important facilities such as care centers and hospitals must be maintained. New forms of transportation are a supplement to this, for example the 'automaatjes' project, where volunteers transport people without cars for a small fee.

215896.explanation: This does not apply to roads that connect districts and villages. 30-kilometer zones must be clearly indicated. Around primary schools, 20-kilometer zones and a no-stopping rule during the start and end of the school day will be introduced. Enforcement is essential. More pedestrian crossings and safe cyclist crossings must be created.

215899.explanation: There are many schemes available for people with too low income. As long as wages and benefits are too low to live independently, these schemes remain necessary. Many people are unaware of their existence and need help with this.

215900.explanation: To prevent loneliness and maintain social cohesion in neighborhoods and villages, community centers and care centers in neighborhoods are indispensable. Small-scale locations ensure accessibility is low-threshold.

215842.explanation: Your opinion counts. Not only when you vote but also afterward. For major plans, you should be able to co-decide. That can be done with a referendum. Then you can say yes or no to a plan. BVNL believes that is fair. You live here, so you decide on your neighborhood and your municipality.

215844.explanation: Why should a small group decide for you? That is not fair. You can perfectly say what you think yourself. BVNL chooses the referendum. Then everyone can participate, not just a few people who are selected by lot. Your opinion is just as important. Real participation is participation for everyone.

215845.explanation: You work hard for your money. Then the municipality should not ask for more and more. The property tax (ozb) has been too high for years. BVNL wants the municipality to cut costs first. Not from you, but from unnecessary expenses. The money you earn is yours. Lower charges mean more space for your family or business.

215847.explanation: Who decides on your municipality? That should be the municipal council, not a group of officials from multiple municipalities. In cooperative arrangements, control disappears and your influence decreases. BVNL wants Roosendaal to decide for itself. That is clearer. And then you know who to hold accountable.

215848.explanation: Not a total ban, but a critical look. We want companies that offer real jobs for Roosendaal residents. Not just big box stores with few staff. The municipality may set conditions but should not reject companies outright. Entrepreneurs are welcome, provided it benefits our residents.

215849.explanation: Empty shops make the city center dull. That is bad for the entrepreneurs who are there. BVNL wants the municipality to help, not with extra rules but by offering opportunities, such as temporary shops for starters. This keeps the center lively and gives new entrepreneurs a chance.

215850.explanation: Just want to buy something in the city? Then you don't want to pay for parking right away. With the first hour free, you step into the store more easily. Good for you, and good for the retailer. BVNL wants the city center to be alive. That starts with fewer barriers. Free parking helps. And it costs you nothing extra.

215851.explanation: A campaign sounds nice. But it costs your tax money. Companies don't come because of a beautiful flyer. They come when the rules are simple and the burdens are low. BVNL wants the municipality to work on that. Not on expensive advertising. Make Roosendaal attractive, then companies will come naturally.

215852.explanation: Roosendaal already has many business parks. They aren't even full yet. BVNL believes we should first use that space. And not for big boxes, but for real companies where you can work. We don’t have to build everything up. Green and open space are also important for our municipality.

215853.explanation: You may have been on the waiting list for a house for years. Then it is not fair if someone else gets priority. BVNL believes: everyone equal. No priority for anyone. You live and work here. You pay taxes. Then you should get the same chance to get a house. That is simply fair.

215854.explanation: Integration is important. But you have to do it yourself. Learn the language. Participate in the neighborhood. Work. BVNL believes the municipality should not spend extra money on this. You pay taxes. That money should go to things that help you. Whoever comes here to live is responsible for belonging themselves.

215855.explanation: Roosendaal already does a lot. We have shelter for Ukrainians. And temporary shelter for asylum seekers. The municipality also deals with nuisance and crime. That puts pressure on our neighborhoods. BVNL says: enough is enough. Solve our own problems first. We can't keep taking on more.

215857.explanation: Roosendaal is located close to Belgium. Coffeeshops attract people from across the border. That causes nuisance and crime. You don't want that. The coffeeshops are closed for a reason. BVNL wants to keep it that way. Your neighborhood must be safe. No drug tourism in our municipality.

215859.explanation: You already pay for your waste through taxes. So why pay again at the recycling center? That is double. And if it becomes too expensive, people dump their stuff in nature. Nobody wants that. BVNL says: make the recycling center free. That’s fair. And it keeps our municipality clean.

215860.explanation: A street with trees and plants is nicer to live in. It looks beautiful. You have shade when it’s warm. BVNL finds greenery in the neighborhood important. Not because of climate demands. But simply because it’s pleasant. When a street is redesigned, greenery should be included.

215863.explanation: Trees make our municipality more beautiful. They provide shade and are nice to see. If a tree has to be cut down, a new one must be planted in its place. That’s logical. BVNL wants to preserve greenery. Not by complicated rules. But by something simple: if you cut down a tree, you plant a new one. Done.

215865.explanation: Roosendaal has the HogeNood app. Businesses open their toilets for free. That works. We rose from rank 282 to 17. BVNL says: make the app more known, don’t build more toilets. Smart cooperation with entrepreneurs is better than spending extra money. The current system works well.

215866.explanation: Agricultural land is for growing food. Not for covering with solar panels. Large companies buy up land for solar parks. That comes at the expense of our farmers and landscape. BVNL says: solar panels belong on roofs, not on meadows. Protect our farmland and food supply.

215867.explanation: Children who do sports and culture grow up healthy. They learn to cooperate and make friends. That is good for them and for the neighborhood. BVNL thinks the municipality may support this. Not with expensive projects, but by helping clubs. So every child can participate, even if parents have little.

215868.explanation: Exercise is healthy. But you don’t need a municipal coach for that. You know yourself if you want to play sports. BVNL believes: give the money to sports clubs. Not to extra municipal positions. Strong clubs help more than another new project. Fewer civil servants, more volunteers.

215869.explanation: The Netherlands is a country full of water. Ditches, canals, and swimming pools. Knowing how to swim can save lives. Not every child gets the chance to learn at home. BVNL believes all children should be able to swim. School swimming helps with that. This is not a luxury. This is safety. The municipality may contribute.

215870.explanation: A beautiful municipality is nice to live in. Art can contribute to that. Think of a nice statue in a square or an artwork that fits Roosendaal’s history. BVNL supports this. But the art must be something residents can be proud of. No strange projects, but something beautiful for everyone.

215871.explanation: A pop venue costs a lot of money. Not only to build, but also to maintain. That is not the municipality’s task. Your tax money should go to basics: safety, maintenance, care. If there is enough demand, an entrepreneur will come naturally. The municipality doesn’t have to arrange everything itself.

215872.explanation: Education is important. But it’s not a municipal task. That is the national government’s responsibility. The municipality should spend your tax money on things that really matter. Clean streets. Safety. Good healthcare. If a school wants to settle here, that’s great. But not with municipal money.

215873.explanation: Safety in your neighborhood is important. The municipality is responsible for that. More enforcement officers on the street ensure rules are followed. They tackle nuisance and are visible in the neighborhood. BVNL sees safety as a core task. The municipality may invest in this. You must feel safe where you live.

215874.explanation: You are the boss of your own life. The government should not watch everywhere. More cameras mean more control. BVNL chooses enforcement officers on the street. Real people who can help you. Not cameras tracking you. Safety is important, but not at the cost of your freedom and privacy.

215875.explanation: You have the right to peace in your own home. Whoever causes trouble must feel the consequences. Higher fines show: this is not allowed. Your freedom ends where another’s begins. BVNL stands for personal responsibility. Cause nuisance? Then you bear the consequences. It’s that simple.

215876.explanation: This concerns our own people. Roosendaal residents who have fallen into hardship. That can happen to anyone. BVNL believes: we do not let our residents sleep on the streets. Good shelter helps people get back on their feet. Not endless shelter, but a helping hand to continue on their own. That is true care.

215877.explanation: Enforcement officers belong on the street. Not behind a desk. Two fixed locations cost money and keep officers off the street. BVNL wants visible enforcement officers in the neighborhood, not in offices. One base is enough. The rest of the time, they must be outside. Where you need them. Walking, not sitting.

215878.explanation: You live here. You work here. You pay taxes here. Then you should be first in line for a house in Roosendaal. Not someone from outside who just comes by. BVNL believes: local residents first. That is not exclusion, that is logical. Your municipality, your chance for a house.

215880.explanation: Your building, your choice. The municipality should not levy extra taxes. BVNL is against higher burdens. Vacancy is solved by removing rules, not by punishing. Make conversion easier. Then owners will get to work automatically. No punishment, but space. That works better.

215881.explanation: There is a housing shortage. Young people and families from Roosendaal cannot find a home. Then we must dare to build. There is space on the edges of the city and villages. BVNL says: allow those houses. Build for our own people. Don’t endlessly wait for complicated plans. Just build where possible.

215882.explanation: New houses are already being built energy-efficiently. That is regulated nationally. The municipality does not need to add extra rules. That only makes building more expensive and complicated. BVNL says: no extra obligations. The national government already takes care of it. Let the municipality focus on fast construction.

215883.explanation: Young people in Roosendaal cannot find a house. Elderly want to downsize, but there is nothing. If we build smartly, we help both groups. And family homes become available. BVNL believes: build for our own people. Young and old. That’s not steering, that’s logical. This helps everyone.

215884.explanation: BVNL wants affordable housing. But mandating a percentage doesn’t work. It scares off builders and makes projects more expensive. Then you get fewer homes. Let the market build. Make sure it can happen fast. The more homes, the better for everyone. Coercion doesn’t help, giving space does.

215886.explanation: Working is better than sitting at home. Some people want to work but just can’t manage. A helping hand supports them to stand on their own feet. That is not charity, that is investing in our own people. BVNL believes: give people a chance. Work provides dignity, income, and participation.

215887.explanation: A benefit is help from all of us. Those who can and want to contribute should get that chance. Volunteering or a neighborhood job keeps you active and gives satisfaction. BVNL believes: participation is important. Not as punishment, but as an opportunity. Good for yourself and fair to those who work.

215888.explanation: A job must be real, not dependent on subsidies. When the subsidy stops, the job stops. That helps no one. BVNL wants to guide people towards real work. Help entrepreneurs with fewer rules; then they hire people. That is better than artificial jobs the municipality keeps paying for.

215889.explanation: Debt relief sounds social, but it keeps people poor. As soon as you earn a bit more, you lose everything. That is the poverty trap. BVNL doesn’t want band-aids, but real solutions. Lower taxes for everyone. That way you help people forward instead of keeping them dependent on government.

215890.explanation: Now you have to go to different counters for help. That’s complicated and time-consuming. For you and the municipality. BVNL wants it simpler. One budget, one application, one overview. Less bureaucracy, fewer civil servants, less hassle. That helps people faster. And saves the municipality money.

215891.explanation: You decide how to travel. By car, bike, or train. The municipality does not need to build expensive fast bike routes to get you cycling. BVNL sees this as no core task. It costs a lot of money better used for maintenance or safety. You choose how you go to work yourself.

215893.explanation: Expensive parking drives away visitors. That hurts businesses in the city center. If the municipality lacks money, it should cut elsewhere. Not make citizens pay more. BVNL says: a lively city center pays off in the end. Cheaper parking attracts people. That is better for everyone.

215894.explanation: Sunday shopping exists to keep the city center alive. Parking must not be a barrier then. A low fixed rate attracts more visitors. That is good for you and the shopkeeper. BVNL believes: make it attractive to come. That keeps the center vibrant. Lower costs, more people, more turnover.

215895.explanation: Elderly in small villages deserve help to stay independent. A dial-a-ride brings them to town or doctor. BVNL finds this important. The budget must be clear and monitored. Care for our elderly, but wisely with money. No blank check, but a warm heart.

215896.explanation: 30 km/h everywhere? That goes too far. By schools and playgrounds we understand. But not on every street. We want rules where really needed for safety. Not the same rule everywhere. People can think for themselves. Trust the citizen; don’t make rules everywhere.

215899.explanation: A help desk is only useful if people know it exists. Many residents with money problems don’t know the way. Good information helps them find the help that already exists. Important: the campaign must reach the right people. Not just a flyer, but real contact.

215900.explanation: Meeting places are important against loneliness. For elderly, youth, and anyone seeking connection. Community centers belong to this. The municipality can help with the building and basic support. But a community center becomes stronger if it also runs itself: with rentals, clubs, and volunteers.

215842.explanation: For important issues, participation and consultation should take place beforehand so that the opinions of residents are taken into account. Moreover, council members are already democratically elected by the residents. Therefore, referendums are not necessary.

215844.explanation: In fact, all residents should have the opportunity to advise the municipality, not just a select group.

215845.explanation: Roosendaal is an expensive municipality in terms of municipal taxes. In addition, there have been too many budget overruns in recent years. By managing these better, the property tax (OZB) can be reduced.

215847.explanation: Due to all these collaborations, the municipality is losing grip and control. Taking matters into our own hands allows for better management. We are in favor of cooperation, if it contributes to the welfare of the residents of Roosendaal and the villages.

215848.explanation: The municipality must provide more space for small-scale and high-quality businesses. This is good for the diversity of employment. Moreover, box-like buildings 'pollute' our landscape.

215849.explanation: Vacancy promotes decay. Therefore, vacant shops should be used differently, possibly temporarily. Temporary shops are an option, but in cases of structural vacancy, residential use can also be chosen.

215850.explanation: Those who come for a quick errand should not immediately get a high parking bill. Moreover, this measure attracts more people to the city center.

215851.explanation: A good location sells itself. An expensive campaign is not necessary. Primarily provide good infrastructure and an attractive environment to do business.

215852.explanation: Existing industrial sites should be 'refreshed' where necessary and first further completed before sacrificing scarce space for this function.

215853.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit essentially have the same status as other registered people seeking rental housing. Therefore, there is no need for any priority. The waiting time for regular housing seekers is already extremely long.

215854.explanation: Integration only succeeds when newcomers speak the language well and adapt to their new environment. This requires efforts from the municipality. The municipality also has a connecting role between companies and newcomers. The costs will pay off.

215855.explanation: Roosendaal has many problematic neighborhoods that require many efforts and investments (NPR). An asylum seekers' center (AZC) hinders this process. Moreover, no suitable locations for large-scale reception have been found. Small-scale reception in limited numbers is the only way to help genuine refugees.

215857.explanation: The misery and decay of the city center during the coffee shop era were enormous. This must never happen again. So, no more coffee shops. The municipality has a role model function and must promote healthy behavior among young people. Extra control on drug sales and more enforcement are needed.

215859.explanation: The number of waste dumping and overflow at containers is enormous. Cleaning up costs a lot of money. By making a limited amount of residual waste per household free to dispose of, dumping is prevented and the city and villages are kept cleaner.

215860.explanation: A green living environment is healthy, attractive, and better for the environment. More plants and trees in the city are important for cooling, improving air quality (capturing fine dust), water management (water absorption during rain), and the health and well-being of residents.

215863.explanation: Replanting trees ensures the preservation of nature, biodiversity, and quality of life. We feel responsible for a green and healthy environment and want to leave this to future generations as well.

215865.explanation: Clean, freely accessible public toilets contribute to an accessible, hospitable, and inclusive municipality. This increases comfort for visitors, elderly people, and the disabled. A basic requirement is that these are clean and safe.

215866.explanation: Agricultural land is primarily intended for food production and a vital rural area. Solar panels do not contribute to this. Where agreements have already been made, they must be respected. New projects are better located on roofs and other suitable locations.

215867.explanation: Sports and culture give children equal opportunities, stimulate their development, strengthen social bonds, help discover talents, and prevent future health and social problems. By involving more children, we build a strong and healthy future for our youth.

215868.explanation: Neighborhood sports coaches can stimulate health and physical activity in an accessible way. At the same time, we continue to look for other smart solutions and collaborations to get as many residents moving as possible.

215869.explanation: In our water-rich country, swimming is important for safety. School swimming helps ensure all children learn to swim. But many families arrange swim lessons themselves. We advocate accessible swimming lessons for everyone, with customized solutions when needed. This does not necessarily have to be school swimming.

215870.explanation: More art in public spaces enriches the city and villages, stimulates creativity and cultural experience, and makes the environment more attractive.

215871.explanation: A pop venue in Roosendaal offers young people from the city and villages a place for music and activities, entertains them positively, and reduces boredom and unrest.

215872.explanation: Higher professional education (HBO) enriches Roosendaal as an educational city. A fine provision that binds more young people to the city and will raise the current average educational level of our youth, with all positive consequences.

215873.explanation: More municipal enforcement officers increase safety, ensure compliance with rules, and help prevent nuisance. This makes residents feel safer and enhances livability in neighborhoods and safety in public spaces.

215874.explanation: Camera surveillance helps prevent nuisance and crime. We also support enforcement and supportive policies to prevent crime and nuisance. Privacy must be protected with camera surveillance. Cameras should only be placed where absolutely necessary.

215875.explanation: Noise nuisance is very burdensome for residents. Higher fines encourage rule compliance. Festivals and events are very welcome; good agreements about noise must be made during permitting.

215876.explanation: The number of homeless people is growing. Additional shelter provides homeless people with safety, basic provisions, and a chance to improve their own circumstances. A roof over their heads with good guidance helps people participate in society again. More shelter reduces nuisance.

215877.explanation: A fixed location also makes enforcement officers more accessible and enforcement more visible. We also want enforcement officers to be clearly visible and present in the city and villages.

215878.explanation: We are committed to residents of Roosendaal and the villages who are looking for their own home and want to continue living in the municipality. Waiting lists must be shortened.

215880.explanation: Vacancy must be countered as much as possible. Regarding taxation on vacancy, we believe that agreements should be made to take into account the circumstances and reasons for vacancy.

215881.explanation: We stimulate housing construction to create more homes for residents. We favor building on the edges of the city and villages while preserving green spaces and focus on housing construction in the center where space becomes available.

215882.explanation: We will not force homeowners to build energy-neutral. New homes must remain affordable. However, energy-neutral building can be encouraged through subsidies and tax benefits for energy-saving measures.

215883.explanation: New homes are needed for all target groups. The municipality must have a clear overview of the demand in the housing market to build tailor-made. Smaller homes and apartments for young people and elderly are certainly needed, while also considering the overall housing need.

215884.explanation: Currently, the standard in Roosendaal is that 30% of newly built homes are social rental homes. This seems sufficient at the moment. If it becomes clear that this percentage is insufficient, it can be adjusted as needed. First, have a good picture of the housing demand.

215886.explanation: Residents should be encouraged as much as possible to work. The municipality can stimulate employers to hire people with distance to the labor market through wage cost subsidies. Ultimately, this costs the community less money because fewer benefits are paid.

215887.explanation: By doing socially meaningful work, not only is the individual encouraged to participate more actively in society, but societal organizations such as foundations are also helped by these extra volunteers. But only if the person can handle it physically and mentally.

215888.explanation: Residents should be encouraged as much as possible to work. The municipality can stimulate employers to hire long-term unemployed people through wage cost subsidies. Ultimately, this costs the community less money because fewer benefits are paid.

215889.explanation: If you can demonstrate that you are unable to meet your tax obligations, you should remain eligible for exemption. The municipality should not be the cause of more debts in families and must instead prevent people from falling into debt.

215890.explanation: List De Regt wants more tailored solutions for people with low incomes who need help. Not every (family) situation is the same. By offering more tailored support, the municipality provides exactly what is needed and neither more nor less.

215891.explanation: If funding is available, express cycling routes are a great asset for our municipality. This way, cycling for long distances is encouraged. Good agreements with the relevant neighboring municipalities are important in this regard.

215893.explanation: By making paid parking in the city center cheaper, people are encouraged to shop and stay longer and more frequently in the center.

215894.explanation: Lijst De Regt stands for a welcoming city. People go to the city to relax and meet each other.

215895.explanation: Customized public transport is flexible and supplementary transport in neighborhoods/villages. If there is a need for it, the municipality can financially support this.

215896.explanation: In residential neighborhoods and home zones where many families live or close to schools, the maximum speed can be reduced to 30 km/h. In other places, the normal limit of 50 km/h can be maintained.

215899.explanation: Lijst De Regt wants to combat poverty through more preventive help and easily accessible support. An information campaign is needed to encourage people to seek help earlier. However, a campaign alone is not enough. The municipality must also be able to provide quick and adequate assistance afterwards.

215900.explanation: Our elderly deserve attention and cozy meeting places to combat loneliness.

statements.title: There must be more neighborhood sports coaches to promote health and physical activity.
statements.moreInfo: In Roosendaal neighbourhood sports and movement coaches work to get residents to exercise and be more active. These coaches operate through Sportservice Noord-Brabant (SSNB). They organise activities at schools, in neighbourhoods and with organisations for the elderly and people with disabilities.
statements.pro: Neighbourhood sports coaches help residents become more active and live healthier lives. They also reach groups who are less likely to exercise, such as older people and youth. More exercise can help reduce healthcare costs.
statements.con: Extra neighborhood sports coaches cost the municipality a lot of money. Sports are primarily the responsibility of individuals. The municipality should focus on basic facilities, not extra sports coaching.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
statements.moreInfo: Sometimes the municipality fells trees, for example because of disease, safety or construction plans. A municipality has rules for cutting down and replanting trees.
statements.pro: Trees create a pleasant, green neighborhood. They help prevent too much paving and asphalt. Trees are good for health.
statements.con: A fixed rule for every tree is too strict. Residents and entrepreneurs should be able to make their own choices. Tailored solutions are better than one fixed rule.

statements.title: The municipality should handle more itself and participate less in partnerships with other municipalities.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality has many tasks. For that reason many Dutch municipalities cooperate with other municipalities. Aldermen and civil servants then consult with each other. A decision they make together applies to all participating municipalities. In Roosendaal this happens in various ways. For example, Roosendaal cooperates with other municipalities in the urban region West‑Brabant West. This cooperation mainly concerns economic and spatial developments. In addition Roosendaal cooperates with other municipalities in areas such as care and support, safety, taxation, ICT and the environment.
statements.pro: The municipality can decide for itself when it carries out tasks in-house. The municipal council can better monitor and control what happens. The municipality is closer to residents.
statements.con: Working with other municipalities saves money. A municipality cannot do everything alone. Cooperation brings more knowledge and experience.

statements.title: A group of residents selected by lottery should advise the municipality on important issues.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality can involve residents in decisions about important topics. One way to do this is through a citizens' assembly. In a citizens' assembly a group of residents is chosen by lottery. This group reflects society, for example in age and place of residence. The participants talk together about a topic and then give advice to the municipality. The municipal council ultimately decides what to do with that advice.
statements.pro: It increases residents' involvement in decisions. A citizens' assembly can provide careful advice on difficult topics. Other municipalities have had positive experiences with such assemblies.
statements.con: The elected municipal council should make decisions, not citizens chosen by lot. Not all residents can participate or have a voice. There is a risk the group would not be a good reflection of society.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.moreInfo: Newcomers are people who move to the Netherlands, for example refugees or labour migrants. They often have to build a new life in a different environment. The municipality helps newcomers, for example with learning Dutch, finding work, following education and participating in society.
statements.pro: Good integration helps newcomers participate in society more quickly. Integration helps newcomers find work and become independent. Good integration creates more mutual understanding in society.
statements.con: Integration is primarily the responsibility of newcomers themselves. The municipality already spends enough on integration. Extra money will not automatically solve the problems.

statements.title: Dropping off residual waste at the recycling center must be free.

titleParts.glossary: Waste that remains after paper, plastic and organic waste have been collected separately.
A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.moreInfo: Residual waste is waste that remains after separating items such as paper, plastic and GFT (vegetable, fruit and garden waste). In Roosendaal residents can dispose of residual waste at the recycling centre. For disposing of residual waste you currently pay €0.24 per kilogram.
statements.pro: Free drop-off can prevent illegal dumping and litter. Cleaning up litter costs the municipality a lot of money. Free drop-off ensures a cleaner living environment.
statements.con: Free drop-off increases costs for the municipality. Charging encourages people to separate waste better. With a paid system the waste tax remains low.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.moreInfo: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, ozb) is a tax people pay if they own a house or another building. How much you pay depends on the value of your house or building and the rates set by the municipality. The revenue from the property tax is used by the municipality for things such as roads, green spaces and support for residents.
statements.pro: A lower property tax reduces housing costs for homeowners. This forces the municipality to work more efficiently. The municipality must be frugal with taxpayers' money.
statements.con: The property tax (ozb) is needed to carry out municipal tasks. A reduction means less money for, for example, care. The municipality would then have to raise other taxes or cut spending.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.moreInfo: People can apply for asylum in the Netherlands if they are fleeing war or are in danger in their own country. The Immigration and Naturalisation Service (IND) assesses their application and decides whether they may stay in the Netherlands. During that procedure these asylum seekers must be accommodated. There is a shortage of reception places for asylum seekers in the Netherlands. According to the Distribution Act the municipality of Roosendaal must provide accommodation for 300 asylum seekers for a period of four years. The municipal council decided in April 2025 not to allow accommodation in Heerle. As a result the municipality does not meet the agreement and the national government can impose accommodation.
statements.pro: The municipality is obliged to accommodate asylum seekers. A reception centre is needed because there are too few places. With a reception centre the municipality can decide how to organize the reception.
statements.con: A reception center for asylum seekers (azc) has major consequences for the neighborhood. Not all residents support the arrival of an azc. The municipality should be able to make its own choices, without coercion from the national government.

statements.title: The municipality must make the first hour of parking free at shops.
statements.moreInfo: In shopping areas visitors often have to pay to park. The municipality determines where and when paid parking applies. In Roosendaal on-street parking is charged Monday through Saturday from 9:00 AM. On Sunday paid parking applies from 12:00 PM. In parking garages you always have to pay.
statements.pro: Free parking attracts more visitors. It helps local businesses. It makes the town centre more attractive.
statements.con: Parking fees are necessary for municipal revenue. Free parking can lead to more cars and congestion. A low rate for short-term parking is a better alternative.

statements.title: The municipality must invest in more shelter spaces for homeless people.
statements.moreInfo: Homeless people are those without a fixed place to live. They sleep, for example, on the street, in night shelters or with others. The municipality is responsible for social shelter for the homeless. In Roosendaal Kloosterstraat 1 has been designated as a temporary shelter location. People who are homeless or temporarily without housing can go there. This shelter is intended for about 1.5 to 2 years. During that period the municipality is looking for a permanent location for social shelter.
statements.pro: Shelter gives people the prospect of rejoining society. More shelter places are needed because the number of homeless is increasing. More shelter prevents nuisance.
statements.con: More shelter places do not structurally solve homelessness. There is already sufficient funding for homeless shelters. The focus should be on prevention and housing, not just on shelter.

statements.title: Within built-up areas, the maximum speed must be reduced to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: An area with buildings (houses, other structures).
The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.

statements.moreInfo: The built-up area is the part of the city or village where people live, work and shop. A speed limit of 50 km/h often applies there now. On some streets it is already 30 km/h. The Mobility Vision Roosendaal 2040 states that 30 km/h should become the norm for almost all roads within the built-up area. Only on large, busy roads intended for through traffic will 50 km/h remain. These are roads where cyclists and cars are separated.
statements.pro: 30 km/h makes traffic safer. A lower speed reduces noise. Residential areas become quieter.
statements.con: 30 km/h leads to slower traffic and reduced flow. It worsens accessibility. On through roads 50 km/h should remain in effect.

statements.title: The municipality must invest in a promotional campaign to market Roosendaal as an attractive location for businesses.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in een

titleParts.glossary: A series of advertisements on, for example, billboards, television, and social media.

titleParts.text: voor de gemeente Roosendaal als aantrekkelijke

titleParts.glossary: The location where a company establishes itself.

statements.moreInfo: Municipalities can attract companies by showing what they have to offer. This can be done, for example, with a promotional campaign. The goal is to convince companies to settle in the municipality.
statements.pro: A promotional campaign can attract more companies. More companies create more jobs in the municipality. It strengthens the local economy.
statements.con: A promotion campaign costs a lot of money. Companies settle primarily because of good facilities, not advertising. There are already enough companies in the municipality.

statements.title: There must be more art in public spaces.

titleParts.text: Er moet meer kunst komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Outdoor areas that are accessible to everyone, such as streets, squares, and parks.

statements.moreInfo: Art in public space is art that is visible and accessible to everyone. You find this art in parks, on squares, in the street and also on, at or in buildings. Examples include statues, facade stones, paintings and graffiti murals. But the music played by the city carillonneur and the poems of local poets are also forms of art.
statements.pro: Art makes neighborhoods more attractive and lively. Art can create pride and recognition in the municipality.
statements.con: Art is not a priority for the municipality. We can better spend municipal money on other tasks.

statements.title: The municipality must primarily focus on building smaller homes and apartments for young people and the elderly.
statements.moreInfo: There are too few homes in Roosendaal. Young people, starters and the elderly in particular have difficulty finding suitable housing. The municipality wants more movement in the housing market. That means people move to a home that better suits their situation, freeing up homes for others.
statements.pro: Small homes are more affordable for young people and first-time buyers. They help older people to live appropriately on one level. More small homes improve circulation on the housing market.
statements.con: The municipality should build for all target groups, not mainly for one group. Family homes are still needed for families with children. Too many small homes can create unbalanced neighborhoods.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.moreInfo: School swimming is swimming lessons that pupils receive through school. The goal is for children to learn to swim and be able to keep themselves safe in the water. In the past school swimming was offered in many municipalities. Nowadays parents often arrange this themselves. As a result, not all children obtain a swimming certificate.
statements.pro: School swimming helps children learn to swim safely. Swimming lessons through school help reduce inequality between children. School swimming makes better use of pools.
statements.con: Swimming lessons are primarily the responsibility of parents. School swimming costs the municipality a lot of money. Schools should focus mainly on subjects like math and reading, not on swimming lessons.

statements.title: The municipality of Roosendaal must allow coffeeshops again.

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.moreInfo: In the Netherlands the use and sale of soft drugs is permitted under conditions. Municipalities decide for themselves whether to allow coffeeshops. In 2009 Roosendaal introduced a new coffeeshop policy and chose a zero policy. This means coffeeshops are not permitted in the municipality. That policy still applies.
statements.pro: Coffeeshops provide supervision of sales. This can help reduce street dealing and nuisance. Residents can buy soft drugs in a controlled way.
statements.con: Coffeeshops have caused nuisance and crime in the past. Coffeeshops can lead to drug tourism. The municipality should promote healthy behavior.

statements.title: Owners of properties that remain vacant for a long time should be required to pay a tax.
statements.moreInfo: A municipality can introduce a vacancy tax for homes or commercial buildings that have been unused for more than a year. The aim of this tax is to encourage owners to rent out or sell the buildings. In Roosendaal the vacancy rate is 4%. This is higher than the provincial average in North Brabant of 2%.
statements.pro: The tax encourages owners to use their buildings. Less vacancy is good for streets and neighborhoods. Less vacancy helps address the housing shortage.
statements.con: Owners do not always have influence over vacancy. The municipality should talk and help, not punish. Because of taxes, owners have less money to renovate their buildings.

statements.title: The municipality must expand fast-cycle routes, for example to Etten-Leur and Essen.

titleParts.glossary: Bicycle paths with few intersections and side paths, on which cyclists can travel long distances quickly and safely.

titleParts.text: uitbreiden, bijvoorbeeld naar Etten-Leur en Essen.

statements.moreInfo: Fast cycling routes are wide and safe cycle paths for longer distances. They are mainly intended for daily cycling, for example to work or school. In Roosendaal there is already a fast cycling route from Roosendaal to Bergen op Zoom. There are currently no concrete plans to extend it towards Etten-Leur. However, the province has expressed a desire to expand fast cycling routes further.
statements.pro: Fast bike routes make cycling safer and more attractive. More people can use the bike instead of the car. Fast bike routes improve accessibility between municipalities.
statements.con: Building fast bicycle routes costs a lot of money. Not everyone uses these routes. Improving public transport yields more benefit.

statements.title: Refugees with a residence permit must not be given priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
Rental homes with a low rent (in 2026 this is an amount of at most € 932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.moreInfo: Refugees with a residence permit are people who applied for asylum and are allowed to stay in the Netherlands. These are status holders. The national government determines every six months how many status holders a municipality must house. This number is based on the municipality's population. Municipalities may decide themselves who gets priority for a social rental home. Status holders do not automatically receive priority; that depends on the municipality’s rules.
statements.pro: Priority for status holders is not fair to other housing seekers. Waiting times should be the same for everyone. Priority rules can make it harder for young people and starters to find a home.
statements.con: Priority should remain possible for vulnerable groups, such as status holders (recognized refugees). We would rather build more social housing to shorten waiting lists than remove priority. Good reception and integration of refugees helps maintain oversight and control in the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality must require that new homes be built energy-neutral.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet verplichten dat nieuwe woningen

titleParts.glossary: An energy-neutral municipality generates as much clean energy as all its residents and businesses together consume. A building or area can also be energy-neutral.

statements.moreInfo: An energy-neutral home generates as much energy as it uses. This is achieved, for example, with good insulation, solar panels and efficient systems. New homes already must meet national rules for energy-efficient construction. Municipalities can set additional requirements for new builds, for example to make homes completely energy-neutral.
statements.pro: Energy-neutral homes lead to lower energy costs for residents. They help use renewable energy and reduce CO₂ emissions. New homes are thus ready for the future.
statements.con: Energy-neutral building makes homes more expensive. Strict requirements can lead to fewer and slower housing projects. The municipality should make building easier, not impose extra rules.

statements.title: There must be more camera surveillance in the municipality of Roosendaal.

titleParts.text: In de gemeente Roosendaal moet meer

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.moreInfo: Municipalities may use cameras to monitor and enforce public order. The police may then view and use the footage. In Roosendaal the mayor has decided to place cameras in the city centre. These are mainly near nightlife areas and in places with drug-related nuisance.
statements.pro: Camera surveillance helps tackle nuisance and crime. Cameras increase the feeling of safety. Footage can assist in criminal investigations.
statements.con: Camera surveillance affects residents’ privacy. Cameras do not solve the causes of nuisance. We should use surveillance temporarily and carefully.

statements.title: There should be more subsidized jobs for the long-term unemployed in our municipality.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer gesubsidieerde banen voor

titleParts.glossary: People who have been without paid work for a long time (longer than a year).

statements.moreInfo: Long-term unemployed are people who have been without paid work for a long time. Subsidised jobs are jobs in which the municipality pays part of the wage.
statements.pro: Subsidized jobs help people get back to work. They prevent people from needing benefits for a long time. Work provides more structure and self-confidence.
statements.con: Subsidized jobs cost the municipality a lot of money. People can remain dependent on the government. Work should mainly come through ordinary jobs with employers.

statements.title: Fines for noise nuisance must be increased.
statements.moreInfo: Noise nuisance occurs when people or companies make a lot of noise, such as loud music or machines during renovations. Municipalities have rules for this in the General Local Regulation (APV). If someone does not comply with these rules, the municipality can impose a fine. The amount of the fine is mainly determined by national rules; the municipality cannot decide this entirely on its own. The municipality can choose stricter enforcement and may apply a higher fine within the bounds of the law.
statements.pro: Stricter fines can reduce noise nuisance. Higher fines make clear that we do not accept noise disturbances. Fines protect livability and peace.
statements.con: The fines are already high enough. Noise nuisance is mainly solved with education and clear rules. The problem is primarily enforcement, not the level of the fine.

statements.title: The municipality must invest more in tailored public transport, for example a dial-a-ride bus.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld steken in openbaar

titleParts.glossary: A type of public transport that doesn't run on a fixed schedule but can be called to pick you up.
A bus that only runs if you reserve it in advance. The bus then takes you door to door.

statements.moreInfo: On-demand public transport is transport that only runs when someone needs it. Examples are a dial-a-ride bus or shared taxi. The municipality of Roosendaal is part of Deeltaxi West‑Brabant. This service is for people who, due to illness, disability or old age, cannot travel independently or use regular public transport. The shared taxi runs only by reservation and is intended as a supplement to bus and train. There is also on-demand public transport for everyone via BravoFlex, which transports passengers from a local stop to a larger public transport interchange.
statements.pro: Tailored public transport ensures that everyone can participate in society. It makes villages more accessible. It is a good complement to bus and train services.
statements.con: Demand-responsive public transport costs a lot of money. This is more a provincial task. We can better use the money for improved bus and train services.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
statements.moreInfo: Solar panels generate sustainable energy. They can be installed on roofs or on open land. Sometimes they are placed on agricultural land, which is land intended for farming and food production. The municipality can set rules about where solar panels are allowed.
statements.pro: Solar panels are better placed on roofs and business parks. Agricultural land is needed for growing food. Solar panels on farmland disrupt the landscape.
statements.con: Solar panels on agricultural land are needed to generate sufficient sustainable energy. This allows many households to use clean and affordable energy. Farmers should be allowed to choose how they use their land.

statements.title: The municipality must open a fixed location for enforcement officers (boa's) in the town center and at the station.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet een vaste locatie voor

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.
Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: ) openen in het centrum en bij het station.

statements.moreInfo: Enforcement officers (BOAs) supervise and enforce rules in public spaces. They address people causing nuisance, check compliance with rules and work with the police. The municipal council of Roosendaal decided on 16 May 2024 to temporarily house enforcement at Laan van Brabant 60-64.
statements.pro: A fixed location makes enforcement officers more accessible to residents. Community officers can supervise busy places more quickly and effectively. The visible presence of officers increases the feeling of safety.
statements.con: Enforcement officers should mainly be present on the street. A fixed location costs the municipality extra money. Deployment should focus on enforcement where needed, not on a permanent site.

statements.title: The municipality must invest in an information campaign about the help desk for people in poverty.
statements.moreInfo: The Roosendaal Resident Support desk helps residents with financial worries and poverty. Residents can get help with debts, benefits and poverty schemes, receive advice and be referred to other forms of support.
statements.pro: More information ensures that more residents know how to find help. Early assistance can prevent bigger problems. Programs only work well if people know they exist.
statements.con: We can better spend the money on direct help to residents. A campaign can increase shame. Information campaigns do not always reach the people who need help.

statements.title: There must be a single budget for low-income people who need assistance.

titleParts.glossary: A sum of money for a specific task or assignment.

titleParts.text: komen voor mensen met een laag inkomen die hulp nodig hebben.

statements.moreInfo: People with low incomes sometimes have trouble paying bills or making ends meet. The municipality of Roosendaal has various schemes to help them, such as special social assistance or debt support. The municipality may choose to combine this help into a single budget.
statements.pro: One budget is clear and transparent. With one budget, help reaches the right people faster. Early help can prevent debt and bigger problems.
statements.con: Tailored solutions through multiple schemes work better than a single budget. There are already several schemes for people with low incomes. Extra assistance can cause people to remain dependent on support.

statements.title: The municipality must invest in meeting places for the elderly.
statements.moreInfo: Some older people have less contact with others or feel lonely. The municipality of Roosendaal works with organizations such as WijZijn, Groenhuysen, GGZ WNB, CultuurCompaan and Sportservice Noord-Brabant to organize meetings and social contact, for example through activities like Stamtafels, the Odensehuis and the Herstel XL Academy in neighborhoods and villages.
statements.pro: Meeting places help prevent loneliness. They ensure that older people stay active and can participate in society.
statements.con: Older people mainly need an incentive to get out more, not necessarily new meeting places. There are already neighborhood centers and clubs where older people can meet.

statements.title: The municipality must allow housing development on the edges of the city and villages.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality of Roosendaal must build new homes. This can be within the existing towns and villages, but also on the outskirts of towns and villages.
statements.pro: At the edges there is more space to quickly build homes. It helps address the housing shortage. Young people can thus remain living in their own village or neighborhood.
statements.con: We must first build homes within the city and villages. Building on the outskirts harms nature. We should first convert vacant buildings into housing.

shootoutStatements.title: There should be more public toilets in the municipality of Roosendaal.
When redesigning streets and squares, there should be more space for plants and trees.
The municipality should actively combat vacancies in the city center, for example with temporary shops.
More business parks/industrial sites should be created in the municipality of Roosendaal.
Paid parking in the center of Roosendaal should be made cheaper.
The municipality should invest more money in activities to involve children in sports and culture.
For important issues, the municipality should consult Roosendaal residents by referendum.
The municipality should invest in expanding higher education in Roosendaal.
There should be a pop music venue in Roosendaal.
The municipality of Roosendaal should have more municipal enforcement officers (boa’s) focused on safety.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.
Roosendaal should invest more in projects that help people who face barriers to the labor market find work or internships.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.
At least 40% of new homes built in Roosendaal should be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.
On shopping Sundays, parking garages should have a fixed low parking rate.
The municipality should no longer allow large logistics companies.
